value.found_quote: Choose the approval mode that matches your comfort level before Codex edits or runs commands.
value.rationale: The page references "Approval modes" — a feature that lets users choose how much control they have before Codex edits or runs commands. This aligns with the concept of a "Plan-then-execute" mode where the agent produces a reviewable plan before making changes. While the page does not use the exact phrase "Plan-then-execute," the description of approval modes (choosing a mode "before Codex edits or runs commands") directly substantiates the existence of a mode in which users can review what Codex intends to do before it acts. The feature appears to be generally available (active).
